reviews.review_title: Login working now but trial over
reviews.review_description:
Can't log in after creating a new account. I purchased this ages ago for my samsung Ativ500t Windows 7 tablet and loved it. Thaught I'd give the VR version a try. Guess even this one is outdated as well. I'm sure it was nice if anything like the original. Glad I didn't have to buy this first to find out.
Thank you for the reply, but even tho the log in works now, the trial has ended and not paying for something if I don't know if it works or not. Maybe next trial version will function completely. I'd be using for just personal experience, not a professional or doctor here. Limited budget so have to be certain of where I spend my money.

developer_response.body: please contact us on at support@irusu.co.in so that we can help you on your queries.

reviews.review_description:
This is an amazing educational tool! The VR visuals of the human body are incredibly detailed, and navigating through different systems (muscles, bones, organs) feels intuitive and immersive. Great for students, teachers, or anyone curious about anatomy. Highly recommend this for learning in a fun and interactive way! 

Really useful anatomy experience with good visuals and easy-to-use controls. I enjoyed exploring different body parts and learning through immersion. A few UI elements could be smoother, but overall a solid educational VR app.

The Hearts left ventricle was on the right hand side and connected to the pulmonary artery.. I would probably understand if it was just labelled incorrectly, but it was the thicker ventricle. I stopped playing at that point. Would be very confusing for a student.
Plus sides are the visuals are very appealing, and it is great that you can pull everything apart. The direct function is also good.

Venas and Arterias are not correct Latin words. It's either Vena/Arteria for singular and Venae/Arteriae for plural. Funny Latin translation attempt but in several parts the translation is incomplete (a few english words are left within the Latin translated text) or in some cases the translator recognises the word as being english and actually pronounces it in english instead of Latin, for example the word functiones should be correctly pronounced funk-tee-own-ace but the narration pronounces it as "functions".
Extra star for the pass through! Shame I had to rate so low, but faux Latin spelling is a no-go.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed feedback, and we sincerely apologize for the errors in the Latin translation. We truly appreciate you pointing these out and will make it a priority for our team to correct the terminology and ensure the translation and narration are consistent and accurate. We'll also work on refining the pronunciation to avoid these issues in the future. Your insights are invaluable, and we hope to improve based on your suggestions. We’re glad you enjoyed the pass-through feature, and we’ll strive to provide a better experience next time. Thank you again for your patience!

reviews.review_description: Really enjoying the app for learning anatomy. Very well executed, super engaging, thanks so much folks!

developer_response.body: Thank you and glad you liked it.

reviews.review_title: Very difficult to enter login credential
reviews.review_description: I had troubles activating the keyboard of meta quest 3. Needed multiple clicks in every text input field to get the keyboard open.. Couldn't figure out how to use right controller for anything but clicking on the panel attached to the left controller. Not able to change height of the body as I wanted to keep sitting. Not possible to move around the body using thumbsticks. Couldn't grab any organ to examine it. Couldn't change the poses as the logo of the app shows the body having arm up. I was hoping for different poses to explore for example knees at different angles. ACL rupture and such. Making the body more transparent should somehow allow for background not to intervene with it. The void background with transparency shows passthrough behind the organs. It became very confusing. It looks great otherwise. To even log in to the app.

Bringing this app into my classroom has changed the game. It's one thing to talk about the human body; it's another to explore it together in 3D. My students are more engaged, and we're all having fun learning
My Sister is studying in medical field and I bought this app for her and She find this app very useful. She is enjoying this app very much and found it very helpful with her studies.

reviews.review_title: Best app so far for learning human anato
reviews.review_description: Must try app for learning human anatomy. All the parts are shown in detail
I must say this application will make a significant impact in Education sector. I recommend it for medical students. Must try once.
